channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Navigating the pressures facing CIOs
item.description: According to consulting firm Korn Ferry,[1] the average CIO is in their role for just 4.6 years. That’s shorter than most of their boardroom peers and significantly less than CEOs’ average tenure of 6.9 years.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The US agency that monitors weather will cut another 1,000 jobs, AP sources say
item.description: Multiple sources tell The Associated Press that the Trump Administration is starting another round of job cuts — this one more than 1,000 — at the nation’s weather, ocean and fisheries agency.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Future PlayStation games could have AI-powered characters, if this leaked prototype of Aloy is anything to go by
item.description: AI-powered characters could feature in future PlayStation games, and the Horizon series could be the testbed with an AI Aloy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ransomware gangs allegedly hit two major US healthcare firms, 300,000 patients have data stolen
item.description: Sunflower Medical Group and Community Care Alliance confirmed suffering a cyberattack as Rhysida claims responsibility.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cómo saber si un proceso empresarial está listo para la IA agéntica
item.description: Este podría ser el año en que la inteligencia artificial (IA) agéntica llegue a lo más alto, ya que muchas empresas buscan casos de uso de valor añadido.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Two-thirds of Silicon Valley tech workers are foreign-born, new report reveals
item.description: Of tech workers with a bachelor's degree or higher, 23% came from India and 18% from China, while 17% were born in California, according to the Silicon Valley Index.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: DOGE deploys AI chatbot at GSA amid workforce reductions
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), backed by Elon Musk, has expanded the deployment of its custom AI-powered chatbot, GSAi, to approximately 1,500 government workers at the General Services Administration.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Shock, horror – I’m not going to argue with Microsoft’s latest bit of nagging in Windows 11, as this pop-up is justified
item.description: Windows 11 is getting yet another pop-up, but I’ll give this particular bit of nagging a pass as it could be useful.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cybertruck owners are joining a Facebook support group—and want to be a protected class
item.description: Cybertruck owners are having a tough time of it, increasingly saying that their $80,000-plus vehicles are the victims of cheese-related attacks and “anti-Nazi” harassment in response to Tesla CEO Elon Musk’s mass-firings role as head of the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration halts funding for two cybersecurity efforts, including one for elections
item.description: The Trump administration has cut millions of dollars in federal funding from two cybersecurity initiatives, including one dedicated to helping state and local election officials.
item.dc:creator: Christina A. Cassidy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk outlines DOGE work in rare TV interview
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk offered new details about the work of President Trump’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) on Monday, while doubling down on the group’s efforts amid mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Wyoming billionaire 1 of 4 in race to buy TikTok
item.description: Wyoming entrepreneur Reid Raisner told "NewsNation Live" on Monday that the fate of TikTok is a matter of U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: AI slop is suffocating the web, says a new study
item.description: The generative AI revolution shows no sign of slowing as OpenAI recently rolled out its GPT-4.5 model to paying ChatGPT users, while competitors have announced plans to introduce their own latest models—including Anthropic, which unveiled Claude 3.7 Sonnet, its latest language model, late last month
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: X outage affects thousands of users
item.description: An outage on the social platform X appeared to impact thousands of users on Monday morning. According to outage-tracking website Downdetector.com, more than 37,000 users reported problems with the platform shortly before 10 a.m. EDT on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: 3 simple ways to fight back against spam calls
item.description: There’s a special place in you-know-where for spam callers. They’re annoying. They waste time. They’re also dangerous. And while it’s challenging to eliminate spam calls entirely, the good news is that with a little time and effort, you can significantly reduce the number of spam calls you receive.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok’s ‘recession brunette’ trend signals tough economic times
item.description: Noticed all the blondes going back to their natural hair color lately? As much as many try to claim it’s because of a “hair health journey,” other factors seem to be at play here. “How’s the economy?” one TikTok user asks in a viral video.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: iDEAL Semiconductor CEO decries CHIPS Act loss, warns of tech talent gap
item.description: In his address to Congress last week, US President Donald J. Trump renewed his criticism of the CHIPS Act, the initiative aimed at reshoring the semiconductor industry, calling it “horrible.” He also called for any remaining funds to be returned to taxpayers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Building generative AI? Get ready for generative UI
item.description: There’s currently no stopping generative AI spending. Analyst firm Omdia expects spending on generative AI applications to hit $58 billion by 2028. Yet while this level of investment is a significant amount, actual deployment of generative AI applications is still slow.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 things developers love about JavaScript – and 10 things they don’t
item.description: It was 30 years ago today, Sgt. JavaScript taught the web to play. Well, not exactly but close enough. The programming language began in 1995 as a project in the depths of the browser maker Netscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vibes won’t make your software successful
item.description: I regret to inform you that vibes don’t pay the bills. Specifically, open source vibes. For as long as I’ve followed open source, there has been a tendency for the industry to focus on normative values of open source, i.e.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: February’s IT job market in the US shows mixed signals; unemployment ticks up
item.description: “Sluggish” might be the best word to describe the IT job market after the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published its data for February employment today. The data offered mixed signals for US tech jobs: even though the number of tech roles grew, unemployment rose.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Duckduckgo launches its version of an AI search assistant
item.description: After nearly two years of intensive beta testing, Duckduckgo has launched the live version of the AI assistant previously known as Duck Assist. Similar to Google’s AI Overviews, the AI Assistant can provide quick answers to users’ questions, such as why Pluto is no longer classified as a planet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Stop carrying your physical driver's license and add it to your Apple Wallet
item.description: A digital ID may be even more secure than a physical ID. Storing your driver's license in your Apple Wallet is an ideal solution for commuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, remains committed to Juniper buy, faces tariff issues
item.description: After sharing a mostly positive revenue report for the first quarter of its 2025 fiscal year, HPE executives detailed a number of challenges the company will face in the coming months, including layoffs, a court case over its proposed buy of Juniper Networks, and the U.S. government’s tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5
